q: The normal force exerted by a banked road is parallel to the road.
The banking angle is directly proportional to the radius of the path.
For every banked curve, the frictional force between the tyres and the road is not important.
In a banked curved road, cars can drive at any speed.
In a banked curve, the vertical component of the normal force is the centripetal force.
The banking angle is directly proportional to the mass of the car.
The force that enables an object to keep it moving in the circle is the tangential force.
Without the centripetal force, the object would not move in a circular path.
For uniform circular motion, tangential speed is constant.
The vertical component of the normal force provides centripetal force in a banked road.
The banking angle is directly proportional to the limiting speed.
When an aeroplane tilts, the horizontal component of the lifting force acts normally to the motion of the aeroplane.
In a banked curved road, the angle that the inclined track makes with the vertical is called the angle of banking.
The lifting force acts parallel to a flying aeroplane body.
Banking of roads is raising the inner edge of the curved road higher than the outer edge.
In the absence of the centripetal force, the object would not move along a circle.
For the banked curved road, the frictional force is not essential.
For every banked curve, there is a limiting speed.
When an aeroplane tilts, the horizontal component of the lift force is the tangential force.
In the absence of the centripetal force, the object would move in a straight line.
The curved road must be banked so that the cars make a safe turn without skidding.
The directional component of centripetal acceleration is tangential to the circular path.
There is no limiting speed in a banked curve.
When a car moves in a curved circular path, the vertical component of the normal force is the required centripetal force.
There are different forms of forces which can provide centripetal force.
For uniform circular motion, tangential velocity is constant.
The centrifugal force is needed for an object to move in a circle.
The curved road must be banked to prevent the skidding of the cars.
For a banked curve, the centripetal force is the horizontal component of the normal force.
The greater the mass of the car, the smaller the limiting speed for a certain banking angle.
The gravitational force of the sun acting on the Earth causes the Earth to move in a circular path.
